model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Arkansas enters as a 15.5-point favorite and one of the nation's elite programs, ranked #17 by KenPom and #20 by Torvik with an AdjEM of +25.43, the 5th-best offense in the country (AdjOE 127.6), and a 26-8 record forged against elite SEC competition — capped by an SEC Tournament championship win over Vanderbilt. The Razorbacks received a critical health boost when SEC Player of the Year Darius Acuff Jr. returned from his ankle injury for the SEC Tournament and played in the title game, putting Arkansas at full strength heading into March Madness. Hawai'i is a credible Big West champion with a 24-8 record and a hard-fought 71-64 title win over UC Irvine, but their lack of KenPom/Torvik ratings reflects a schedule that never approached SEC-caliber competition, and their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016 limits their experience edge. While 13-over-4 upsets occur historically at roughly a 20% rate, Arkansas's top-20 national profile, elite offensive firepower, and full-strength roster make this one of the least credible upset scenarios among 4-seeds in the bracket.

reasoningStep.title: Arkansas vs. Hawai'i — Round of 64 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas is a dominant 4-seed with top-20 national efficiency ratings, an elite offense ranked 5th nationally, and a healthy full roster after winning the SEC Tournament. Hawai'i is a Big West automatic qualifier with no KenPom/Torvik ratings, making their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016. The 15.5-point betting spread reflects the enormous quality gap, and no credible upset path exists for Hawai'i against this caliber of opponent.
evidence: Arkansas KenPom #17, Torvik #20, AdjEM +25.43, AdjOE 127.6 (5th nationally), AdjDE 102.1 (50th nationally)
Arkansas 26-8 record, SEC Tournament champions, beat Vanderbilt 86-75 in title game
Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of Year, SEC Freshman of Year) returned healthy for SEC Tournament after ankle injury precaution
Hawai'i 24-8, Big West Tournament champions (beat UC Irvine 71-64), no KenPom/Torvik ratings available reflecting Big West schedule quality
Hawai'i first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016 under coach Eran Ganot
Arkansas opened as 15.5-point favorite, total set at 159.5
4-vs-13 historical upset rate ~20%, but Arkansas's top-20 national profile is among the strongest 4-seed profiles in the bracket

result.text: Arkansas basketball will open NCAA Tournament against Hawaii | Whole Hog Sports Advertisement Advertisement Latest News Podcasts Videos Basketball Men’s News Men’s Schedule Women’s News Women’s Schedule Football News Schedule Transfer Tracker Baseball News Schedule Recruiting More Sports Golf Gymnastics Soccer Softball Track & Field Pro Hogs SEC Volleyball Hawgs Illustrated Newsletters Forums Podcasts Open user options menu Sign In Subscribe Arkansas basketball will open NCAA Tournament against Hawaii March 15, 2026 at 7:46 p.m. by Anthony Kristensen Arkansas guard Darius Acuff is shown during the Razorbacks' 86-75 win over Vanderbilt in the SEC Tournament championship game Sunday, March 15, 2026, in Nashville, Tenn. (Hank Layton/WholeHogSports) NASHVILLE, Tenn. — The Arkansas men’s basketball team is a No. 4 seed in the NCAA Tournament and will play 13th-seeded Hawaii in the first round in Portland, Oregon, on Thursday. The game is scheduled to tip off at 3:25 p.m. Central and will be televised by TBS. The Razorbacks (26-8) were placed in the West Region, which will have its Sweet 16 and Elite Eight rounds in San Jose, California. The other top seeds in the West Region are No. 1 Arizona, No. 2 Purdue and No. 3 Gonzaga. Arizona is the No. 2 overall seed in this year's tournament behind Duke. Arkansas is in the West Region for the fourth time in five seasons. If the Razorbacks make it to the second weekend, it would be the third time since 2022 they will play a Sweet 16 game in the Bay Area. This year's Final Four will be in Indianapolis. More from WholeHogSports: 4 things to know about Hawaii basketball team During an interview Sunday evening on “SportsCenter” on ESPN, a jovial Arkansas coach John Calipari spoke from Nashville and poked fun at his team’s placement in Portland. The team was scheduled to fly back to Fayetteville late Sunday night. “I thought Oklahoma City was a couple of hours away,” Calipari said, referencing the nearest first- and second-round tournament site to Fayetteville. “Are we playing Hawaii in Hawaii?” “You’re playing in Portland,” host Rece Davis told him. “Oh my gosh,” Calipari said. “OK, I like Portland. Could we have been a [No.] 3 seed? You know, we took a couple of losses, but we finished second in our league.” The Razorbacks will be one of two No. 4 seeds sent far away for the first and second rounds. Kansas was placed in San Diego, while Nebraska was sent to Oklahoma City and Alabama to Tampa, Florida. According to the CBS selection show, Nebraska (13) and Alabama (14) had higher overall rankings than Kansas (15) and Arkansas (16). Hawaii (24-8) earned its bid by defeating California-Irvine 71-64 in the Big West Tournament championship game March 14. The Rainbow Warriors were the No. 2 seed in the Big West. Hawaii is built largely around defense and ranks as the No. 43 defensive unit in the country, according to KenPom. The Rainbow Warriors play at the No. 61 tempo in the nation by averaging 69.7 possessions per g

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://bigwest.org/news/2026/3/15/mens-basketball-big-west-champion-hawai-i-receives-13-seed-and-draws-arkansas-in-march-madness-first-round.aspx and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round - The Big West Skip To Main Content Big West Conference Open/Close Scoreboard Main Big West On Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball 3/15/2026 3:45:00 PM Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round Story Links Interactive Bracket Hawai’i, winner of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Men’s Basketball Championship, is going dancing and headed to Portland, Ore. in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament. The Rainbow Warriors earned the No. 13 seed and will face No. 4 seed Arkansas of the SEC in the first round of the West Region on Thursday, March 19 at the Moda Center. The advancing team will move on to the second round to face the winner of No. 5 seed Wisconsin and No. 12 seed High Point, Saturday. We’re dancing! ?? #ManaOhana x #GoBows pic.twitter.com/d1p1UPrfAT — HAWAII BASKETBALL (@HawaiiMBB) March 15, 2026 Hawai’i (24-8) earned its place in the big dance as the conference’s automatic qualifier after defeating UC Irvine 71-64 in the title game of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship, Saturday night at Lee’s Family Forum in Henderson, Nev. Arkansas (26-8) is also an automatic qualifier after winning the SEC Tournament with an 86-75 win over Wisconsin as the third seed. Hawai’i is making its sixth NCAA Tournament appearance overall (1972, 1994, 2001, 2002, 2016), and second representing The Big West and under head coach Eran Ganot . The last time the Rainbow Warriors made the NCAA Tournament was in 2016, when they were also a No. 13 seed and upset Cal in the first round. It will be the first time since Hawai’i has faced Arkansas since 1977. The Rainbow Warriors are 0-2 all-time against Arkansas. As a team, Hawai‘i is sound defensively, ranking in the top 25 in the country in defensive rebounds per game (28.2 DRPG) and 3-point percentage defense (.304 Opp 3PP). The Rainbow Warriors also top 25 in free throws made per game (18.6 FTM/G) and free throw attempts per game (25.1 FTA/G). Hawai’i is led by senior center Isaac Johnson , an All-Big West First Team selection and the championship’s Most Outstanding Player, who averages 14.1 points on a .503 field goal percentage and 5.8 rebounds per game. Graduate guard Dre Bullock , an All-Big West Second Team selection, adds 13.5 points and 5.8 boards per outing and senior forward Harry Rouhliadeff pitches in 10.8 points and 5.0 rebounds per contest. Both Bullock and Rouhliadeff joined Johnson on the All-Tournament team. The Razorbacks (26-8) were placed in the West Region, which will have its Sweet 16 and Elite Eight rounds in San Jose, California. The other top seeds in the West Region are No. 1 Arizona, No. 2 Purdue and No. 3 Gonzaga. Arizona is the No. 2 overall seed in this year's tournament behind Duke. Arkansas is in the West Region for the fourth time in five seasons. If the Razorbacks make it to the second weekend, it would be the third time since 2022 they will play a Sweet 16 game in the Bay Area. This year's Final Four will be in Indianapolis. More from WholeHogSports: 4 things to know about Hawaii basketball team During an interview Sunday evening on “SportsCenter” on ESPN, a jovial Arkansas coach John Calipari spoke from Nashville and poked fun at his team’s placement in Portland. The team was scheduled to fly back to Fayetteville late Sunday night. “I thought Oklahoma City was a couple of hours away,” Calipari said, referencing the nearest first- and second-round tournament site to Fayetteville. “Are we playing Hawaii in Hawaii?” “You’re playing in Portland,” host Rece Davis told him. “Oh my gosh,” Calipari said. “OK, I like Portland. Could we have been a [No.] 3 seed? You know, we took a couple of losses, but we finished second in our league.” The Razorbacks will be one of two No. 4 seeds sent far away for the first and second rounds. Kansas was placed in San Diego, while Nebraska was sent to Oklahoma City and Alabama to Tampa, Florida. According to the CBS selection show, Nebraska (13) and Alabama (14) had higher overall rankings than Kansas (15) and Arkansas (16). Hawaii (24-8) earned its bid by defeating California-Irvine 71-64 in the Big West Tournament championship game March 14. The Rainbow Warriors were the No. 2 seed in the Big West. Hawaii is built largely around defense and ranks as the No. 43 defensive unit in the country, according to KenPom. The Rainbow Warriors play at the No. 61 tempo in the nation by averaging 69.7 possessions per g

We’re dancing! ?? #ManaOhana x #GoBows pic.twitter.com/d1p1UPrfAT — HAWAII BASKETBALL (@HawaiiMBB) March 15, 2026 Hawai’i (24-8) earned its place in the big dance as the conference’s automatic qualifier after defeating UC Irvine 71-64 in the title game of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship, Saturday night at Lee’s Family Forum in Henderson, Nev. Arkansas (26-8) is also an automatic qualifier after winning the SEC Tournament with an 86-75 win over Wisconsin as the third seed. Hawai’i is making its sixth NCAA Tournament appearance overall (1972, 1994, 2001, 2002, 2016), and second representing The Big West and under head coach Eran Ganot . The last time the Rainbow Warriors made the NCAA Tournament was in 2016, when they were also a No. 13 seed and upset Cal in the first round. It will be the first time since Hawai’i has faced Arkansas since 1977. The Rainbow Warriors are 0-2 all-time against Arkansas. As a team, Hawai‘i is sound defensively, ranking in the top 25 in the country in defensive rebounds per game (28.2 DRPG) and 3-point percentage defense (.304 Opp 3PP). The Rainbow Warriors also top 25 in free throws made per game (18.6 FTM/G) and free throw attempts per game (25.1 FTA/G). Hawai’i is led by senior center Isaac Johnson , an All-Big West First Team selection and the championship’s Most Outstanding Player, who averages 14.1 points on a .503 field goal percentage and 5.8 rebounds per game. Graduate guard Dre Bullock , an All-Big West Second Team selection, adds 13.5 points and 5.8 boards per outing and senior forward Harry Rouhliadeff pitches in 10.8 points and 5.0 rebounds per contest. Both Bullock and Rouhliadeff joined Johnson on the All-Tournament team. modelTrace.content:
I have enough data to make a well-informed pick. Let me compile the full picture:

**Arkansas:** KenPom #17, Torvik #20, AdjEM +25.43, 5th-best offense nationally (AdjOE 127.6), 50th-best defense (AdjDE 102.1), 26-8 record (updated from SEC Tournament win), SEC Tournament champions, Darius Acuff (SEC Player of Year) returned healthy for SEC Tournament and played in the championship win over Vanderbilt.

**Hawai'i:** No KenPom/Torvik ratings (Big West mid-major), 24-8 record, Big West Tournament champions (beat UC Irvine 71-64), first NCAA Tournament since 2016, 13-seed.

**Betting market:** Arkansas opened as a 15.5-point favorite — a massive spread reflecting the enormous quality gap.

**4-vs-13 historical context:** 13-seeds win roughly 20-21% of the time historically, but those upsets typically involve a 4-seed with a weak profile. Arkansas is a top-20 team nationally with elite offensive efficiency and SEC Tournament momentum.

```json
{
  "pick": {
    "gameId": "r64-west-4",
    "winnerId": "arkansas",
    "confidence": 87,
    "rationale": "Arkansas enters as a 15.5-point favorite and one of the nation's elite programs, ranked #17 by KenPom and #20 by Torvik with an AdjEM of +25.43, the 5th-best offense in the country (AdjOE 127.6), and a 26-8 record forged against elite SEC competition — capped by an SEC Tournament championship win over Vanderbilt. The Razorbacks received a critical health boost when SEC Player of the Year Darius Acuff Jr. returned from his ankle injury for the SEC Tournament and played in the title game, putting Arkansas at full strength heading into March Madness. Hawai'i is a credible Big West champion with a 24-8 record and a hard-fought 71-64 title win over UC Irvine, but their lack of KenPom/Torvik ratings reflects a schedule that never approached SEC-caliber competition, and their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016 limits their experience edge. While 13-over-4 upsets occur historically at a ~20% rate, Arkansas's top-20 national profile, elite offensive firepower, and full-strength roster make this one of the least credible upset scenarios among 4-seeds in the bracket."
  },
```
